title: /gly
text: /gly Compile glyphs. /gly { /aft= DateTime } { /bef= DateTime } { Library } Switches /aft= or /bef= Parameters Library —name of the library containing the objects to compile; if omitted, all glyphs are compiled. Use wildcard characters to specify multiple libraries. Use With ide.exe —Uniface IDE Description The location of compiled glyphs is determined by the $RSO path. Related Links Compiling from the IDE Command Line The IDE command line can be used to compile multiple objects without opening the IDE. It is especially useful for automating compilation by means of batch scripts. Glyphs Glyphs are images in a Uniface-proprietary format, which can be used as background images in forms, entities and application shells, and as icons in widgets, panel buttons, menus, and drag-and-drop formats.
